claim: There was a critical capability threshold crossed around December 2025 where frontier LLMs shifted from generating isolated code snippets (requiring human supervision) to autonomously producing large, correct functional logic blocks
actionable: If you stopped using AI coding tools before late 2025, revisit them now; the capability jump since then makes autonomous code generation viable for substantial features.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review

claim: Software 3.0 represents the era where LLMs function as universal interpreters: the context window is the new interface and natural language prompts are the control levers, replacing traditional application code layers
actionable: Rethink application architecture for the AI era: instead of building multi-layer systems (frontend → backend → database), design systems where multimodal LLMs can directly accept raw inputs (images, unstructured text) and produce refined outputs end-to-end.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review

claim: In Software 3.0 systems, intermediate application layers (e.g., OCR preprocessing, image generation APIs, database retrieval) become obsolete when advanced multimodal LLMs can perform end-to-end processing directly
actionable: Before building new microservices or data pipelines, test whether a multimodal LLM can solve the problem end-to-end; this often eliminates layers and speeds time-to-market.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review

claim: LLMs are becoming the primary operating system (host process) and CPUs are relegated to coprocessor status, handling only rigid deterministic tasks (calculation, logic gates) when LLM intelligence needs them
actionable: Design future systems with LLMs as the main orchestrator: have the LLM read unstructured inputs, make decisions, and call out to CPUs/calculators/code interpreters as needed for deterministic steps.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review

claim: Frontier LLMs exhibit 'spiked' capability distributions: they reach superhuman performance on verifiable tasks (math, code) but fail at basic common-sense reasoning, creating blind spots that engineering must work around
actionable: When deploying LLMs, identify which tasks are verifiable (code, math) and which are ambiguous (common sense, real-world reasoning); architect monitoring and human gates around the low-confidence areas.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review

claim: Vibe coding democratizes software but lacks rigor; agentic engineering applies professional discipline (monitoring loops, testing gates, quality control) to harness LLMs while maintaining production standards
actionable: Move beyond 'prompt and hope' development: implement verification gates (lint, build, tests, browser verification, critic agents) to ensure agentic systems produce production-quality code.
source_title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering at AI Ascent 2026 — ES bilingual review
